commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR extends Codex app-server timeout classification and rewrites returned Codex error-only payloads into a visible failure reply with regression coverage.

PR surface: Source +9, Tests +34. Total +43 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main only recognizes the old `turn/completed` wording and does not convert returned Codex app-server error payloads through this failure copy; the PR body also supplies a redacted runtime log for the zero-reply failure shape.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow automated repair can add the missing retain-before-fail call and strengthen the new test without changing product direction.

target.title: fix(gateway): surface codex app-server returned failures
target.body:
## Summary

- Surface Codex app-server returned terminal failures as visible reply payloads when the agent produced no normal content.
- Accept the real timeout wording `codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion` in addition to the existing `turn/completed` wording.
- Add regression coverage for the returned-error-payload path so it does not complete silently with zero replies.

## Why

Codex app-server failures are not always thrown from the runner. In timeout cases, the embedded runner can return a completed result with only an error payload such as `codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion` and no visible assistant content. The reply layer only converted a few returned error classes, so this path could fall through as a successful turn with no deliverable reply.

That behavior is confusing for chat channels: the gateway logs show the agent failed, but the user sees no error. This change reuses the existing Codex app-server failure copy and only injects it when there is no non-error outbound content, preserving successful turns and normal session/usage bookkeeping.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Codex app-server timeout returned as an error payload could complete a chat turn with no visible final reply. In the real gateway incident, the Feishu dispatch completed with `queuedFinal=false, replies=0` after `codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion`.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `codex/surface-codex-app-server-returned-failures`, commit `8e4d1669ce`; evidence is from the patched checkout plus redacted runtime log excerpt from the affected OpenClaw setup.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a local terminal probe against the patched checkout using Node to load the current source mapping and feed the returned-error payload text observed in the runtime log (`codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion`).
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

  Terminal output from the patched checkout:

  ```text
  OpenClaw patched checkout real-behavior probe
  commit=8e4d1669ce
  input returned error payload=codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion
  regex matched=true
  non-error content present=false
  visible reply after fix:
  ⚠️ Codex app-server stopped before confirming turn completion. OpenClaw did not replay the turn automatically because it may still be active; try again, or use /new if the session stays stuck.
  ```

  Redacted before-fix runtime log excerpt from the affected setup:

  ```text
  2026-06-16T10:51:47 feishu[feishu2] received user message and dispatched to agent:yuantai:main
  2026-06-16T10:56:46 per-chat task exceeded 300000ms cap and was evicted from queue
  2026-06-16T11:10:45 codex app-server turn idle timed out waiting for completion
  2026-06-16T11:10:45 feishu[feishu2]: dispatch complete (queuedFinal=false, replies=0)
  ```

- Observed result after fix: The same returned-error text now matches the Codex app-server timeout classifier and produces the user-visible Codex app-server failure reply instead of leaving the turn with no visible reply.
- What was not tested: I did not run a live Feishu end-to-end timeout reproduction because that would require intentionally stalling a real Codex app-server session for several minutes. The proof covers the real returned-error shape from the gateway log and the patched reply-layer mapping that prevents the silent completion.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The terminal probe is a local patched-checkout reproduction of the real returned-error path, not a live channel replay. It avoids external model calls and private chat content while using the same error text and final user-visible reply text from the changed code path.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): The redacted runtime log excerpt above shows the pre-fix user-visible failure mode: `dispatch complete (queuedFinal=false, replies=0)` after the Codex app-server completion timeout.

## Validation

- `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts --testNamePattern "Codex app-server"`
- `pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
target.author: litang9

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: litang9
commands.command: please review this pr. real behavior proof has been added and the latest real behavior proof check is passing. the current `security-fast` failure appears unrelated to this pr: it is a production dependency audit finding for existing locked `hono@4.12.21` (`ghsa-88fw-hqm2-52qc`, fixed in `>=4.12.25`). this pr only changes `agent-runner-execution.ts` and its test, and does not touch package manifests or lockfiles.
commands.freeform_prompt: please review this PR. Real behavior proof has been added and the latest Real behavior proof check is passing. The current `security-fast` failure appears unrelated to this PR: it is a production dependency audit finding for existing locked `hono@4.12.21` (`GHSA-88fw-hqm2-52qc`, fixed in `>=4.12.25`). This PR only changes `agent-runner-execution.ts` and its test, and does not touch package manifests or lockfiles.
